description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: Switch is unable to sync with NTP server even though NTP server is reachable and responding to the client. Scenario: In some scenarios when connecting to a NTP server that uses special characters for the reference ID field. The NTP client device will not be able to sync with the server and the error Delimiter for jitter error will be displayed. Workaround: Connect to an NTP server that doesn't uses special characters for the reference ID fields.

description: Symptom: Ops-switchd crash when collecting vxlan diagdump or collecting support files. Scenario: Many access-side LAGs have multiple overlay VLANs configured on them, with corresponding VNI-VLAN mappings under VXLAN. After a system reboot, there may be timing issues with LAG interfaces coming up and access VLAN programming. While this does not cause any immediate impact on its own, issues may arise if VLANs are subsequently removed from the access-side LAGs, along with their corresponding VNIs. In such cases, attempting to collect support files or run the diag-dump vxlan basic command may result in a crash. Workaround: Ensure that LAG interfaces are fully operational before configuring VLANs on them.
symptom: Ops-switchd crash when collecting vxlan diagdump or collecting support files.
scenario: Many access-side LAGs have multiple overlay VLANs configured on them, with corresponding VNI-VLAN mappings under VXLAN. After a system reboot, there may be timing issues with LAG interfaces coming up and access VLAN programming. While this does not cause any immediate impact on its own, issues may arise if VLANs are subsequently removed from the access-side LAGs, along with their corresponding VNIs. In such cases, attempting to collect support files or run the diag-dump vxlan basic command may result in a crash.
workaround: Ensure that LAG interfaces are fully operational before configuring VLANs on them.

Workaround: Ensure that LAG interfaces are fully operational before configuring VLANs on them.

description: Symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted. Scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group. Workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.
symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted.

description: Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

description: Symptom: There is a 30-second delay when updating VLAN access settings with snmpset and immediately checking with snmpget. Scenario: This happens when snmpset changes one OID and snmpget reads another OID before the cache updates. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of snmp.

description: Symptom: When attempting to use SNMP to set the VlanStaticName object, the VLAN name changes temporarily but then reverts back to the original name. Scenario: Attempting to use SNMP set on dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Use the command-line interface (CLI) to change the VLAN name instead of using SNMP set.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
